Harden report issue ingestion: remove fallback issue paths and SVG screenshot fallback (#2184)

* Retain failed reports in queue with retry counter and HITL escalation

Reports now stay on disk until successfully processed. Each failure
increments an attempts counter on the PendingReport model. After 5
failed attempts, the report is removed and escalated to HITL as a
GitHub issue with the raw description, environment, and screenshot
indicator preserved for manual review.

commit.message:
fix(uv): Support arbitrary version specifiers in dependency groups (#828)

## Summary

- Adds `uv/private/versions/` package with a PEP 440 version specifier
evaluator supporting all operators (`==`, `!=`, `>=`, `<=`, `>`, `<`,
`~=`, `===`), compound specifiers, and wildcards
- Replaces `_parse_pinned_version` (which only handled `==` pins) with
`find_matching_version` that evaluates arbitrary specifiers against all
candidate versions from the lockfile
- `normalize_deps` now returns the full `package_versions` map so
multi-version resolution works for all specifier types

This is a follow-up to #819 which fixed `==` pins. Clients reported that
`>=` bounds in `[dependency-groups]` entries cause the same failure. The
new approach is "guess and check" — for each version in the lockfile,
check if it satisfies the specifier.

## Test plan

- [x] 16 new Starlark unit tests for version parsing, comparison, and
all PEP 440 operators
- [x] New e2e test `cases/uv-conflict-gte` with `>=24.0` and
`>=21.0,<22.0` conflicting groups
- [x] `bazel test //...` — 60/60 pass
- [x] `cd e2e && bazel test //...` — 18 pass, 8 skipped (OCI)
- [x] Existing `cases/uv-conflict-817` (== pins) continues to pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: claude <claude@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: aspect-marvin[bot] <marvin@aspect.build>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move default collectors and run_ingestion() into aita-core

Default collectors (collect_handouts, collect_homework, collect_slides,
collect_syllabus) handle the standard directory layout. run_ingestion()
runs the full pipeline. Course repos with standard layout only need:

    from aita_core.ingest import run_ingestion
    run_ingestion(CONFIG)

Custom collectors can be passed for non-standard layouts.

commit.message:
Add instructions for finding Gong owner IDs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(memory): drain stream pipeline before resetting hash config in dispose() (#3007)

* fix(memory): drain stream pipeline before resetting hash config in dispose()

StorageManagerEmulator.close() called cancel() on the consumer session,
which marked it as cancelled but did not terminate the TransformStream
or await completion of the pipeTo() pipeline. This left in-flight
messages in the async stream between consumer and provider, which could
arrive at the provider after Runtime.dispose() reset the canonical hash
config via resetCanonicalHashConfig(). The provider would then compute
legacy hashes for verification while the consumer had signed with
canonical hashes, causing authorization failures.

Changes:
- consumer.ts: Capture the pipeTo() promise as consumer.closed so
  callers can await full pipeline shutdown. Add close() and closed to
  the MemoryConsumer interface.
- cache.deno.ts: Call close() (which cancels + terminates the stream)
  and await closed to ensure all in-flight messages are processed
  before returning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(memory): log unexpected pipeTo errors instead of blanket catch

Replace the empty .catch(() => {}) on the pipeTo() promise with a
handler that checks consumer.isCancelled. Expected teardown errors
(when close()/cancel() was called) are silently suppressed. Unexpected
pipeline errors are logged via the existing logger.error("stream-error")
pattern.

Adds isCancelled readonly getter to MemoryConsumerSession and the
MemoryConsumer interface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(memory): add explanatory comment for pipeTo catch handler

Explains why the catch is needed (teardown terminates the stream,
producing an expected rejection), what it catches (controller.terminate()
rejections during intentional shutdown), and why swallowing the
teardown case is safe (isCancelled gate ensures only expected shutdown
errors are suppressed; unexpected errors are logged).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(memory): rethrow unexpected pipeTo errors instead of logging

Let unexpected pipeline errors propagate to the caller via
`await consumer.closed` rather than catching and logging them.
Only the expected teardown rejection (when isCancelled is true)
is suppressed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(monitoring): add HTTPS reverse proxy for Grafana via monitoring.fitprogress.ovh

- nginx: add monitoring.fitprogress.ovh server block with TLS + Basic Auth
  - HTTP → HTTPS 301 redirect
  - WebSocket support for Grafana Live (Upgrade/Connection headers)
  - proxy_pass to fitprogress_grafana:3000 (shared Docker network)
  - X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN (Grafana uses internal iframes)
  - limit_req zone=api_auth for brute-force protection
- docker-compose.prod.yml: mount ssl-monitoring/ and monitoring.htpasswd into nginx
- docker-compose.monitoring.yml: update GF_SERVER_ROOT_URL to https://monitoring.fitprogress.ovh/
  remove SSH tunnel port (access now via nginx)
- monitoring/prometheus.yml: fix target app:8000 → fitprogress_app_prod:8000
  (cross-stack Docker DNS resolves container names, not service names)
- monitoring/grafana/provisioning/dashboards/: add dashboard provider + FitProgress API dashboard
- scripts/setup-monitoring-access.sh: one-shot idempotent setup script
  (self-signed bootstrap cert → certbot standalone → Let's Encrypt cert → reload nginx)
- .gitignore: exclude nginx/ssl-monitoring/*.pem and nginx/monitoring.htpasswd
- nginx/ssl-monitoring/.gitkeep: track directory so Docker bind mount works on fresh clone

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

opus-2026-03-06-S26: DISPROVE converse of THM-052; position-uniform <=> scalar M at n=5

Key findings:
- Scalar M does NOT imply vertex-transitive (counterexample at n=5: |Aut|=3, scores (1,2,2,2,3))
- Position-uniform (N[v,j] = H/n for all v,j) is EXACTLY equivalent to scalar M at n=3,5
- 64/1024 labeled tournaments on 5 vertices are position-uniform
- Non-VT scalar-M tournament has 3 anti-automorphisms (T -> T^op)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use Watson 1981 TBW formula for personalized Widmark r-value

Replace fixed gender r-values (male=0.68, female=0.55) with the full
Watson et al. 1981 Total Body Water equation that accounts for height,
weight, age and sex. This gives accurate BAC per drink for lean/tall
individuals — e.g. 6'1" 145lb male gets r≈0.78 instead of 0.68,
making the HIGH limit ~5 drinks (not 3) and BAC-per-drink ~13% lower.

Pass height_inches and age through to all calculateBAC/calculateLimits
call sites: useDrinkSession, Profile, Onboarding, CalibrationSurvey.

commit.message:
Fix PPT preview rendering black blocks over slide content

The parseVisuals function was extracting solidFill from anywhere in shape XML,
including text run properties (<a:rPr>), causing text color (#000000) to be
mistakenly used as shape fill. Now only extracts fill from <p:spPr> (shape
properties). Also skips shapes containing visible text (text boxes).

refactor: remove ApexCharts, keep only custom SVG bar chart

Remove apexcharts, react-apexcharts, and html-to-image dependencies entirely.

Wire launch blockers: standings panel, tutorial flow, social onboarding

Remediate the three F-series launch blockers by connecting fully-implemented
systems to the live game flow:

F-016 (Standings UI): Add StandingsPanel with per-player stronghold counts,
doom toll color warnings (≥10), fate deck amber/red thresholds, heartstone
tracking, broken status, and leading-player highlight. Wire into GameController
renderAll() so standings update after every state mutation.

F-012 (Tutorial 5-turn sequence): Wire TutorialState, TutorialEngine, and
TutorialScriptedOpponent into GameController. Tutorial mode initializes the
overlay on start(), routes AI opponent turns through pre-defined node paths
(guaranteeing Turn 3 combat), and advances tutorial turn state on claim (T1),
combat (T3), vote (T4), and forge-keep claim (T5). All tutorial engine calls
use try/catch for headless compatibility.

F-006b (Social Pressure Onboarding): Add social-pressure.css with modal,
scroll area, and disabled-until-scroll dismiss button styles. Add SettingsPanel
gear button (⚙) that re-opens the onboarding from the HUD. Wire
showIfFirstSession() into index.ts after mode selection, skipped in tutorial
mode per PRD.

Add 16 new tests (standings-panel, settings-panel, tutorial integration).
All 874 tests pass, zero type errors.

fix: handle null provider_agent_id/skill_id after agent hard-delete

When agents are permanently deleted, tasks referencing them have their
FK columns set to NULL (ondelete=SET NULL). The TaskResponse schema
required non-null UUIDs, causing a 500 on GET /tasks/.

- Make provider_agent_id and skill_id optional in TaskResponse schema
- Show "Deleted Agent" name for orphaned tasks
- Frontend: handle null agent/skill in task list and detail views
- Hide Retry/Run Again buttons when agent no longer exists

commit.message:
fix: stabilize Cesium creditContainer to prevent viewer recreation

document.createElement('div') in JSX creates a new DOM element on
every render. Resium interprets the prop change as a viewer config
change and destroys/recreates the entire Cesium Viewer. The hooks
(satellites, flights, earthquakes) then held a stale reference to
the destroyed viewer while map points worked via Resium context.

Fix: move creditContainer div to module scope so it's created once.
Also make ref callback always update state if viewer instance changes,
so hooks always get the current viewer.
